output_schema.label: PASS or FAIL
output_schema.rationale: short explanation grounded in source text
output_schema.concerns: empty string if none; otherwise state ambiguity

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: The ACE table stores BooleanExpr at column 0x03 and Columns at column 0x04, and Core permits authorized ACE.Set to modify BooleanExpr. Opal preconfigures ACE_ACE_Set_BooleanExpression with BooleanExpr Admins and Columns BooleanExpr, and K_AES_128/K_AES_256 GlobalRange and Range1 GenKey ACE rows with BooleanExpr Admins and Columns All. Opal also preconfigures the AccessControl rows for setting those ACE objects with ACL ACE_ACE_Set_BooleanExpression, while K_AES key-object GenKey associations use their corresponding K_AES GenKey ACE. Therefore ACE.Get must report the documented cells before personalization and the updated BooleanExpr after successful ACE.Set, Columns must remain the documented All cell, GetACL for the ACE object's Set method must return ACE_ACE_Set_BooleanExpression, and GetACL for the K_AES key object's GenKey method must not be confused with the ACE object's Set ACL.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.2.7.5 ACL This is the access control list for this SP/method, table/method, or object/method combination. This column is modified/accessed via the methods GetACL, RemoveACE, and AddACE. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable directly via the Set method.
5.3.2.7.9 GetACLACL This column holds the access control list that controls invocation of the GetACL method on the ACL column this row in the AccessControl table.

5.3.2.9 Access Control Metadata Group - ACE (Object Table) The ACE table SHALL have one row for each access control element that MAY be referenced in the AccessControl table's ACL column. Table 177 ACE Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|--------------|----------|-----------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | Name | Yes | name | | 0x02 | CommonName | Yes | name | | 0x03 | BooleanExpr | | AC_element | | 0x04 | Columns | | ACE_columns | ```

5.3.2.9.4 BooleanExpr This column value is a Boolean expression of Authorities that authorizes the ACE if the expression evaluates to True. If the conditions described in this access control element are True, then the ACE is considered authenticated.

5.3.3.6 Basic Table Method Group - Get (Table and Object Method) This method is used to fetch the values of selected table cells (see 5.3.4.2.2). TableUID.Get [ ObjectUID.Get [ Cellblock : cell_block ] => [ Result : typeOr { Bytes : bytes, RowValues : list [ ColumnNumber = Value ... ] } ]

5.3.3.7 Basic Table Method Group - Set (Table and Object Method) This method is used to change the values of selected table cells (see 5.3.4.2.6). TableUID.Set [ ObjectUID.Set [ Where = row_address, Values = typeOr { Bytes : bytes, RowValues : list [ ColumnNumber = <type of column> ... ] } ] => [ ]

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.3.1 Result This method returns a list of uidrefs to ACE objects.

5.3.4.3.2 BooleanExpr Column Format The BooleanExpr column of the ACE table has a type of AC_element. Table 210 contains an encoding example of the AC_element list representing the infix ACE expression: ((00 00 00 09 00 00 00 32 AND 00 00 00 09 00 00 00 24) OR 00 00 00 09 00 00 82 73) AND 00 00 00 09 00 00 77 28 Table 210 ACE_expression Encoding Example ```json [ {"Token": "F0", "Meaning": "Start List"}, {"Token": "F2", "Meaning": "Start Name"}, {"Token": "A4 00 00 0C 05", "Meaning": "Half-UID – Authority_object_ref"}, {"Token": "A8 00 00 00 09 00 00 00 32", "Meaning": "Authority_object_ref"}, {"Token": "F3", "Meaning": "End Name"}, {"Token": "F2", "Meaning": "Start Name"}, {"Token": "A4 00 00 0C 05", "Meaning": "Half-UID – Authority_object_ref"}, {"Token": "A8 00 00 00 09 00 00 00 24", "Meaning": "Authority_object_ref"}, {"Token": "F3", "Meaning": "End Name"}, {"Token": "F2", "Meaning": "Start Name"}, {"Token": "A4 00 00 04 0E", "Meaning": "Half-UID – boolean_ACE"}, {"Token": "00", "Meaning": "boolean_ACE - AND"}, {"Token": "F3", "Meaning": "End Name"}, {"Token": "F2", "Meaning": "Start Name"}, {"Token": "A4 00 00 0C 05", "Meaning": "Half-UID – Authority_object_ref"}, {"Token": "A8 00 00 00 09 00 00 82 73", "Meaning": "Authority_object_ref"}, {"Token": "F3", "Meaning": "End Name"}, {"Token": "F2", "Meaning": "Start Name"}, {"Token": "A4 00 00 04 0E", "Meaning": "Half-UID – boolean_ACE"}, {"Token": "01", "Meaning": "boolean_ACE - OR"}, {"Token": "F3", "Meaning": "End Name"}, {"Token": "F2", "Meaning": "Start Name"}, {"Token": "A4 00 00 0C 05", "Meaning": "Half-UID – Authority_object_ref"}, {"Token": "A8 00 00 00 09 00 00 77 28", "Meaning": "Authority_object_ref"}, {"Token": "F3", "Meaning": "End Name"}, {"Token": "F2", "Meaning": "Start Name"}, {"Token": "A4 00 00 04 0E", "Meaning": "Half-UID – boolean_ACE"}, {"Token": "00", "Meaning": "boolean_ACE - AND"}, {"Token": "F3", "Meaning": "End Name"}, {"Token": "F1", "Meaning": "End List"}, ] ```

5.3.4.3.3 Modifying the BooleanExpr Column The BooleanExpr column of the ACE table is modifiable using the Set method. Some implementations MAY allow the BooleanExpr column to be set to an empty list. If the BooleanExpr column value is an empty list, that ACE cannot be satisfied and as such always resolves to False. If an implementation does not permit the BooleanExpr column to be set to an empty list, attempts to do so SHALL result in the Set method failing with a status of INVALID_PARAMETER.

output_schema.label: PASS or FAIL
output_schema.rationale: short explanation grounded in source text
output_schema.concerns: empty string if none; otherwise state ambiguity

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: The ACE table stores BooleanExpr at column 0x03 and Columns at column 0x04, and Core permits authorized ACE.Set to modify BooleanExpr. Opal preconfigures ACE_ACE_Set_BooleanExpression with BooleanExpr Admins and Columns BooleanExpr, and K_AES_128/K_AES_256 GlobalRange and Range1 GenKey ACE rows with BooleanExpr Admins and Columns All. Opal also preconfigures the AccessControl rows for setting those ACE objects with ACL ACE_ACE_Set_BooleanExpression, while K_AES key-object GenKey associations use their corresponding K_AES GenKey ACE. Therefore ACE.Get must report the documented cells before personalization and the updated BooleanExpr after successful ACE.Set, Columns must remain the documented All cell, GetACL for the ACE object's Set method must return ACE_ACE_Set_BooleanExpression, and GetACL for the K_AES key object's GenKey method must not be confused with the ACE object's Set ACL.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.2.7.5 ACL This is the access control list for this SP/method, table/method, or object/method combination. This column is modified/accessed via the methods GetACL, RemoveACE, and AddACE. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable directly via the Set method.
5.3.2.7.9 GetACLACL This column holds the access control list that controls invocation of the GetACL method on the ACL column this row in the AccessControl table.

5.3.2.9 Access Control Metadata Group - ACE (Object Table) The ACE table SHALL have one row for each access control element that MAY be referenced in the AccessControl table's ACL column. Table 177 ACE Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|--------------|----------|-----------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | Name | Yes | name | | 0x02 | CommonName | Yes | name | | 0x03 | BooleanExpr | | AC_element | | 0x04 | Columns | | ACE_columns | ```

5.3.2.9.4 BooleanExpr This column value is a Boolean expression of Authorities that authorizes the ACE if the expression evaluates to True. If the conditions described in this access control element are True, then the ACE is considered authenticated.

5.3.3.6 Basic Table Method Group - Get (Table and Object Method) This method is used to fetch the values of selected table cells (see 5.3.4.2.2). TableUID.Get [ ObjectUID.Get [ Cellblock : cell_block ] => [ Result : typeOr { Bytes : bytes, RowValues : list [ ColumnNumber = Value ... ] } ]

5.3.3.7 Basic Table Method Group - Set (Table and Object Method) This method is used to change the values of selected table cells (see 5.3.4.2.6). TableUID.Set [ ObjectUID.Set [ Where = row_address, Values = typeOr { Bytes : bytes, RowValues : list [ ColumnNumber = <type of column> ... ] } ] => [ ]

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.3.1 Result This method returns a list of uidrefs to ACE objects.

5.3.4.3.2 BooleanExpr Column Format The BooleanExpr column of the ACE table has a type of AC_element. Table 210 contains an encoding example of the AC_element list representing the infix ACE expression: ((00 00 00 09 00 00 00 32 AND 00 00 00 09 00 00 00 24) OR 00 00 00 09 00 00 82 73) AND 00 00 00 09 00 00 77 28 Table 210 ACE_expression Encoding Example ```json [ {"Token": "F0", "Meaning": "Start List"}, {"Token": "F2", "Meaning": "Start Name"}, {"Token": "A4 00 00 0C 05", "Meaning": "Half-UID – Authority_object_ref"}, {"Token": "A8 00 00 00 09 00 00 00 32", "Meaning": "Authority_object_ref"}, {"Token": "F3", "Meaning": "End Name"}, {"Token": "F2", "Meaning": "Start Name"}, {"Token": "A4 00 00 0C 05", "Meaning": "Half-UID – Authority_object_ref"}, {"Token": "A8 00 00 00 09 00 00 00 24", "Meaning": "Authority_object_ref"}, {"Token": "F3", "Meaning": "End Name"}, {"Token": "F2", "Meaning": "Start Name"}, {"Token": "A4 00 00 04 0E", "Meaning": "Half-UID – boolean_ACE"}, {"Token": "00", "Meaning": "boolean_ACE - AND"}, {"Token": "F3", "Meaning": "End Name"}, {"Token": "F2", "Meaning": "Start Name"}, {"Token": "A4 00 00 0C 05", "Meaning": "Half-UID – Authority_object_ref"}, {"Token": "A8 00 00 00 09 00 00 82 73", "Meaning": "Authority_object_ref"}, {"Token": "F3", "Meaning": "End Name"}, {"Token": "F2", "Meaning": "Start Name"}, {"Token": "A4 00 00 04 0E", "Meaning": "Half-UID – boolean_ACE"}, {"Token": "01", "Meaning": "boolean_ACE - OR"}, {"Token": "F3", "Meaning": "End Name"}, {"Token": "F2", "Meaning": "Start Name"}, {"Token": "A4 00 00 0C 05", "Meaning": "Half-UID – Authority_object_ref"}, {"Token": "A8 00 00 00 09 00 00 77 28", "Meaning": "Authority_object_ref"}, {"Token": "F3", "Meaning": "End Name"}, {"Token": "F2", "Meaning": "Start Name"}, {"Token": "A4 00 00 04 0E", "Meaning": "Half-UID – boolean_ACE"}, {"Token": "00", "Meaning": "boolean_ACE - AND"}, {"Token": "F3", "Meaning": "End Name"}, {"Token": "F1", "Meaning": "End List"}, ] ```

5.3.4.3.3 Modifying the BooleanExpr Column The BooleanExpr column of the ACE table is modifiable using the Set method. Some implementations MAY allow the BooleanExpr column to be set to an empty list. If the BooleanExpr column value is an empty list, that ACE cannot be satisfied and as such always resolves to False. If an implementation does not permit the BooleanExpr column to be set to an empty list, attempts to do so SHALL result in the Set method failing with a status of INVALID_PARAMETER.

output_schema.label: PASS or FAIL
output_schema.rationale: short explanation grounded in source text
output_schema.concerns: empty string if none; otherwise state ambiguity

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: The ACE table stores BooleanExpr at column 0x03 and Columns at column 0x04, and Core permits authorized ACE.Set to modify BooleanExpr. Opal preconfigures ACE_ACE_Set_BooleanExpression with BooleanExpr Admins and Columns BooleanExpr, and K_AES_128/K_AES_256 GlobalRange and Range1 GenKey ACE rows with BooleanExpr Admins and Columns All. Opal also preconfigures the AccessControl rows for setting those ACE objects with ACL ACE_ACE_Set_BooleanExpression, while K_AES key-object GenKey associations use their corresponding K_AES GenKey ACE. Therefore ACE.Get must report the documented cells before personalization and the updated BooleanExpr after successful ACE.Set, Columns must remain the documented All cell, GetACL for the ACE object's Set method must return ACE_ACE_Set_BooleanExpression, and GetACL for the K_AES key object's GenKey method must not be confused with the ACE object's Set ACL.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.2.7.5 ACL This is the access control list for this SP/method, table/method, or object/method combination. This column is modified/accessed via the methods GetACL, RemoveACE, and AddACE. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable directly via the Set method.
5.3.2.7.9 GetACLACL This column holds the access control list that controls invocation of the GetACL method on the ACL column this row in the AccessControl table.

5.3.2.9 Access Control Metadata Group - ACE (Object Table) The ACE table SHALL have one row for each access control element that MAY be referenced in the AccessControl table's ACL column. Table 177 ACE Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|--------------|----------|-----------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | Name | Yes | name | | 0x02 | CommonName | Yes | name | | 0x03 | BooleanExpr | | AC_element | | 0x04 | Columns | | ACE_columns | ```

5.3.2.9.4 BooleanExpr This column value is a Boolean expression of Authorities that authorizes the ACE if the expression evaluates to True. If the conditions described in this access control element are True, then the ACE is considered authenticated.

5.3.3.6 Basic Table Method Group - Get (Table and Object Method) This method is used to fetch the values of selected table cells (see 5.3.4.2.2). TableUID.Get [ ObjectUID.Get [ Cellblock : cell_block ] => [ Result : typeOr { Bytes : bytes, RowValues : list [ ColumnNumber = Value ... ] } ]

5.3.3.7 Basic Table Method Group - Set (Table and Object Method) This method is used to change the values of selected table cells (see 5.3.4.2.6). TableUID.Set [ ObjectUID.Set [ Where = row_address, Values = typeOr { Bytes : bytes, RowValues : list [ ColumnNumber = <type of column> ... ] } ] => [ ]

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.3.1 Result This method returns a list of uidrefs to ACE objects.

5.3.4.3.2 BooleanExpr Column Format The BooleanExpr column of the ACE table has a type of AC_element. Table 210 contains an encoding example of the AC_element list representing the infix ACE expression: ((00 00 00 09 00 00 00 32 AND 00 00 00 09 00 00 00 24) OR 00 00 00 09 00 00 82 73) AND 00 00 00 09 00 00 77 28 Table 210 ACE_expression Encoding Example ```json [ {"Token": "F0", "Meaning": "Start List"}, {"Token": "F2", "Meaning": "Start Name"}, {"Token": "A4 00 00 0C 05", "Meaning": "Half-UID – Authority_object_ref"}, {"Token": "A8 00 00 00 09 00 00 00 32", "Meaning": "Authority_object_ref"}, {"Token": "F3", "Meaning": "End Name"}, {"Token": "F2", "Meaning": "Start Name"}, {"Token": "A4 00 00 0C 05", "Meaning": "Half-UID – Authority_object_ref"}, {"Token": "A8 00 00 00 09 00 00 00 24", "Meaning": "Authority_object_ref"}, {"Token": "F3", "Meaning": "End Name"}, {"Token": "F2", "Meaning": "Start Name"}, {"Token": "A4 00 00 04 0E", "Meaning": "Half-UID – boolean_ACE"}, {"Token": "00", "Meaning": "boolean_ACE - AND"}, {"Token": "F3", "Meaning": "End Name"}, {"Token": "F2", "Meaning": "Start Name"}, {"Token": "A4 00 00 0C 05", "Meaning": "Half-UID – Authority_object_ref"}, {"Token": "A8 00 00 00 09 00 00 82 73", "Meaning": "Authority_object_ref"}, {"Token": "F3", "Meaning": "End Name"}, {"Token": "F2", "Meaning": "Start Name"}, {"Token": "A4 00 00 04 0E", "Meaning": "Half-UID – boolean_ACE"}, {"Token": "01", "Meaning": "boolean_ACE - OR"}, {"Token": "F3", "Meaning": "End Name"}, {"Token": "F2", "Meaning": "Start Name"}, {"Token": "A4 00 00 0C 05", "Meaning": "Half-UID – Authority_object_ref"}, {"Token": "A8 00 00 00 09 00 00 77 28", "Meaning": "Authority_object_ref"}, {"Token": "F3", "Meaning": "End Name"}, {"Token": "F2", "Meaning": "Start Name"}, {"Token": "A4 00 00 04 0E", "Meaning": "Half-UID – boolean_ACE"}, {"Token": "00", "Meaning": "boolean_ACE - AND"}, {"Token": "F3", "Meaning": "End Name"}, {"Token": "F1", "Meaning": "End List"}, ] ```

5.3.4.3.3 Modifying the BooleanExpr Column The BooleanExpr column of the ACE table is modifiable using the Set method. Some implementations MAY allow the BooleanExpr column to be set to an empty list. If the BooleanExpr column value is an empty list, that ACE cannot be satisfied and as such always resolves to False. If an implementation does not permit the BooleanExpr column to be set to an empty list, attempts to do so SHALL result in the Set method failing with a status of INVALID_PARAMETER.

output_schema.rationale: short explanation grounded in source text

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: The ACE table stores BooleanExpr at column 0x03 and Columns at column 0x04, and Core permits authorized ACE.Set to modify BooleanExpr. Opal preconfigures ACE_ACE_Set_BooleanExpression with BooleanExpr Admins and Columns BooleanExpr, and K_AES_128/K_AES_256 GlobalRange and Range1 GenKey ACE rows with BooleanExpr Admins and Columns All. Opal also preconfigures the AccessControl rows for setting those ACE objects with ACL ACE_ACE_Set_BooleanExpression, while K_AES key-object GenKey associations use their corresponding K_AES GenKey ACE. Therefore ACE.Get must report the documented cells before personalization and the updated BooleanExpr after successful ACE.Set, Columns must remain the documented All cell, GetACL for the ACE object's Set method must return ACE_ACE_Set_BooleanExpression, and GetACL for the K_AES key object's GenKey method must not be confused with the ACE object's Set ACL.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.2.7.5 ACL This is the access control list for this SP/method, table/method, or object/method combination. This column is modified/accessed via the methods GetACL, RemoveACE, and AddACE. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable directly via the Set method.
5.3.2.7.9 GetACLACL This column holds the access control list that controls invocation of the GetACL method on the ACL column this row in the AccessControl table.

5.3.2.9 Access Control Metadata Group - ACE (Object Table) The ACE table SHALL have one row for each access control element that MAY be referenced in the AccessControl table's ACL column. Table 177 ACE Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|--------------|----------|-----------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | Name | Yes | name | | 0x02 | CommonName | Yes | name | | 0x03 | BooleanExpr | | AC_element | | 0x04 | Columns | | ACE_columns | ```

5.3.2.9.4 BooleanExpr This column value is a Boolean expression of Authorities that authorizes the ACE if the expression evaluates to True. If the conditions described in this access control element are True, then the ACE is considered authenticated.

5.3.3.6 Basic Table Method Group - Get (Table and Object Method) This method is used to fetch the values of selected table cells (see 5.3.4.2.2). TableUID.Get [ ObjectUID.Get [ Cellblock : cell_block ] => [ Result : typeOr { Bytes : bytes, RowValues : list [ ColumnNumber = Value ... ] } ]

5.3.3.7 Basic Table Method Group - Set (Table and Object Method) This method is used to change the values of selected table cells (see 5.3.4.2.6). TableUID.Set [ ObjectUID.Set [ Where = row_address, Values = typeOr { Bytes : bytes, RowValues : list [ ColumnNumber = <type of column> ... ] } ] => [ ]

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.3.1 Result This method returns a list of uidrefs to ACE objects.

5.3.4.3.2 BooleanExpr Column Format The BooleanExpr column of the ACE table has a type of AC_element. Table 210 contains an encoding example of the AC_element list representing the infix ACE expression: ((00 00 00 09 00 00 00 32 AND 00 00 00 09 00 00 00 24) OR 00 00 00 09 00 00 82 73) AND 00 00 00 09 00 00 77 28 Table 210 ACE_expression Encoding Example ```json [ {"Token": "F0", "Meaning": "Start List"}, {"Token": "F2", "Meaning": "Start Name"}, {"Token": "A4 00 00 0C 05", "Meaning": "Half-UID – Authority_object_ref"}, {"Token": "A8 00 00 00 09 00 00 00 32", "Meaning": "Authority_object_ref"}, {"Token": "F3", "Meaning": "End Name"}, {"Token": "F2", "Meaning": "Start Name"}, {"Token": "A4 00 00 0C 05", "Meaning": "Half-UID – Authority_object_ref"}, {"Token": "A8 00 00 00 09 00 00 00 24", "Meaning": "Authority_object_ref"}, {"Token": "F3", "Meaning": "End Name"}, {"Token": "F2", "Meaning": "Start Name"}, {"Token": "A4 00 00 04 0E", "Meaning": "Half-UID – boolean_ACE"}, {"Token": "00", "Meaning": "boolean_ACE - AND"}, {"Token": "F3", "Meaning": "End Name"}, {"Token": "F2", "Meaning": "Start Name"}, {"Token": "A4 00 00 0C 05", "Meaning": "Half-UID – Authority_object_ref"}, {"Token": "A8 00 00 00 09 00 00 82 73", "Meaning": "Authority_object_ref"}, {"Token": "F3", "Meaning": "End Name"}, {"Token": "F2", "Meaning": "Start Name"}, {"Token": "A4 00 00 04 0E", "Meaning": "Half-UID – boolean_ACE"}, {"Token": "01", "Meaning": "boolean_ACE - OR"}, {"Token": "F3", "Meaning": "End Name"}, {"Token": "F2", "Meaning": "Start Name"}, {"Token": "A4 00 00 0C 05", "Meaning": "Half-UID – Authority_object_ref"}, {"Token": "A8 00 00 00 09 00 00 77 28", "Meaning": "Authority_object_ref"}, {"Token": "F3", "Meaning": "End Name"}, {"Token": "F2", "Meaning": "Start Name"}, {"Token": "A4 00 00 04 0E", "Meaning": "Half-UID – boolean_ACE"}, {"Token": "00", "Meaning": "boolean_ACE - AND"}, {"Token": "F3", "Meaning": "End Name"}, {"Token": "F1", "Meaning": "End List"}, ] ```

5.3.4.3.3 Modifying the BooleanExpr Column The BooleanExpr column of the ACE table is modifiable using the Set method. Some implementations MAY allow the BooleanExpr column to be set to an empty list. If the BooleanExpr column value is an empty list, that ACE cannot be satisfied and as such always resolves to False. If an implementation does not permit the BooleanExpr column to be set to an empty list, attempts to do so SHALL result in the Set method failing with a status of INVALID_PARAMETER.
